eyebrow: Make ongoing support prove itself in writing
headline: Build a four-line ledger for monthly value.
subheadline: Rustam will create four rows: question addressed, action changed, rework avoided, and review date. A blank cycle triggers a scope or support reset.
chatStarter: Rustam, I need a simple record showing whether each month changes anything important in my work.
whyItWorks: Four rows turn recurring value into inspectable evidence and expose when support needs correction.

eyebrow: A simple chat record for continuing honestly
headline: Tell me what should earn another month.
subheadline: Tell me the work that mattered. I'll keep a four-line note: question, changed action, avoided rework, review date—and flag an empty month.
chatStarter: Rustam, I think the month helped, but I want a record stronger than a general feeling.
whyItWorks: The first-person invitation makes accountability feel conversational while retaining four concrete value checks.

eyebrow: Put the official story beside what people really mean
headline: Compare polished and candid lines, recover omitted stakes.
subheadline: Paste the polished line beside the candid version. Rustam will label the missing actor, consequence, and constraint, then write the corrected claim.
chatStarter: Rustam, the report says adoption is delayed, while the team says nobody trusts the output with clients.
whyItWorks: Three labels turn vague discomfort into a corrected claim that preserves the real stakes.

eyebrow: The uncomfortable wording may carry useful context
headline: Send me both lines, I'll mark what disappeared.
subheadline: Send the official sentence and the version you almost wrote. I'll circle the changed person, consequence, and constraint, then reflect the fuller meaning back.
chatStarter: Rustam, I wrote that adoption is slow. What I nearly wrote is that our team does not trust it.
proofLine: The original wording reaches Rustam without an intake handoff.
whyItWorks: The reply feels personal while a side-by-side reading keeps the insight grounded in exact wording.
